gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (4/54)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (4/54)
text: سَمِعْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) يَقُولُ لِصَاحِبِ هَذَا الْأَمْرِ غَيْبَتَانِ إِحْدَاهُمَا يَرْجِعُ مِنْهَا إِلَى أَهْلِهِ وَ الْأُخْرَى يُقَالُ هَلَكَ فِي أَيِّ وَادٍ سَلَكَ قُلْتُ كَيْفَ نَصْنَعُ إِذَا كَانَ كَذَلِكَ قَالَ إِذَا ادَّعَاهَا مُدَّعٍ فَاسْأَلُوهُ عَنْ أَشْيَاءَ يُجِيبُ فِيهَا مِثْلَهُ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya and Ahmad Bin Idrees, from Al Hassan Bin Ali Al Kufy, from Ali Bin Hassan, from his uncle Abdul Rahman Bin Kaseer, from Mufazzal Bin Umar who said,

‘I heard Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> saying: ‘For the Master<sup>asws</sup> of this command, there would be two Occultation – (During) one of the two, he<sup>asws</sup> would return from it to his<sup>asws</sup> family, and (during) the other, it would be said, ‘He<sup>asws</sup> perished in whichever valley he<sup>asws</sup> travelled in’. I said, ‘How should one to deal with it, when it would be like that’. He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘When a claimant claims it, so ask him about things. He would answer with regards to these, similar to him<sup>asws</sup>’.
en.sarwar: 20. Muhammad ibn Yahya and Ahmad ibn Idris have narrated from al-Hassan ibn Ali al-Kufi from Ali ibn Hass’an from his paternal uncle, ‘Abd al-Rahman ibn Kathir from Mufaddal ibn ‘Umar who has said the following. “I heard abu ‘Abdallah (a.s.) say, “The in charge of this task (Leadership with Divine Authority) will disappear from the public sight twice. From one them he will return to his family but in the other one it will be said that he is dead or no one knows in which of the valleys has he traveled.” I then asked, “What shall we do in such conditions?” The Imam (a.s.) said, “If any one would claim to have such authority you then should ask him about several things in which he must answer like him (Leader with Divine Authority).”
